item.title: CFTC ‘Watching’ Oil Futures Trading Spikes, Agency Official Says
item.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is monitoring trading in the oil futures market for unusual activity, its enforcement chief said Tuesday.

item.title: Chinese Share Buybacks Surge as Iran War Jolts Global Markets
item.description: China’s listed companies announced the most share buybacks in almost a year last month as the escalating war in Iran drove down stock prices.

item.title: China’s New AI Stocks Drive Volatility in Asia as Hype Grows
item.description: Chinese artificial‑intelligence firms have emerged as one of the most volatile pockets of Asia’s equity markets, with shares of newly listed model developers and chip designers swinging on retail flows.

item.title: Brazil’s Braskem Said to Plan Court Protection Against Creditors
item.description: Braskem SA, Brazil’s largest petrochemical company, is considering seeking protection in local courts against its creditors, according to people involved in the talks.

item.title: South Korea Exports Continue to Surge Despite Escalating Iran Risks
item.description: South Korea’s exports continued to surge in March as robust semiconductor demand helped cushion the economy even as external risks intensify amid the war in Iran.

item.title: Google Readies Screenless Fitbit Band to Rival Growing Whoop Craze
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is developing its own fitness band, aiming to compete with screenless wearable devices from upstarts like Whoop Inc. and Oura Health Oy.

item.title: Japan’s Manufacturer Mood Improves, Buoying BOJ Stance
item.description: Japan’s large manufacturers showed greater confidence in the state of business conditions for a fourth straight quarter even as the Middle East crisis erupted, an outcome that supports the Bank of Japan’s policy stance of continuing to raise interest rates.

item.title: Palmer Luckey-Backed Nuclear Startup Valar Lands $2 Billion Valuation
item.description: Valar Atomics, the nuclear energy startup backed by Anduril Industries Inc. founder Palmer Luckey and Palantir Technologies Inc. executive Shyam Sankar, has raised fresh capital at a valuation of $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: CFTC Top Cop Warns Against Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
item.description: A key Commodity Futures Trading Commission official said the agency will use its powers to root out insider trading in prediction markets, as concerns about suspicious activity on the exchanges grow.

item.title: Iran War Unwinds a Year of Investor Bets on Trump’s Tariffs
item.description: A year since President Donald Trump tripped up global financial markets with his so-called Liberation Day tariffs, the investment playbook his move inspired has been firmly put on the shelf.

item.title: Singapore Weighs Adding Gold Storage for Global Central Banks
item.description: Singapore is considering additional gold storage space to accommodate other central banks’ holdings, as part of a broader push to establish the nation as an international bullion hub.

item.title: Australia Manufacturing Slips to Contraction on War Disruptions
item.description: Australia’s manufacturing sector posted its first contraction in five months in March as demand weakened and cost pressures surged amid the escalating war in the Middle East.

item.title: Optimism for Iran War Offramp Set to Lift Asia Markets
item.description: TEHRAN, IRAN - MARCH 02: A general view of Tehran with smoke visible in the distance after explosions were reported in the city, on March 02, 2026 in Tehran, Iran. (Photo by Contributor/Getty Images)

item.title: Energy Shock Spurs Caution as Asia Bankers Fund Data Center Boom
item.description: The energy shock sparked by the Iran war is increasingly coming up in conversations about data center deals for Asian bankers, who have helped finance billions of dollars of artificial intelligence infrastructure across the region.

item.title: War Fails to Dent Malaysian Shipper’s IPO, Expansion Plans
item.description: MTT Shipping and Logistics Bhd. is forging ahead with expansion plans and an April IPO, shrugging off disruptions from the Middle East war as cargo volumes hold steady.

item.title: Gold Holds Three-Day Gain After Trump Signals End to Iran War
item.description: Gold held three days of gains after President Donald Trump said he expected the US to end the war with Iran within two to three weeks.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Track US Rally on Iran Hopes: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia are poised to track a euphoric Wall Street rally on hopes that the war that’s jolted global markets and disrupted energy supplies may be nearing a conclusion.

item.title: Oil Steadies as Traders Weigh Outlook for Resolution to Iran War
item.description: Oil steadied as traders weighed the outlook for the Middle East war, with the US and Iran signaling openness for a resolution to the conflict that’s caused chaos across global energy markets.

item.title: Microsoft in Talks With Chevron, Engine No. 1 Over $7 Billion Texas Power Plant
item.description: Microsoft Corp. is in exclusive talks with Chevron Corp. and investment fund Engine No. 1 over a long-term deal that would underpin a giant energy complex in West Texas, providing electricity to power a large data center campus.

item.title: New York Sends Jet Fuel to England in Unusual War Trade Reroute
item.description: Two ships carrying jet fuel are headed from New York to England as the war in Iran throws global aviation fuel supply into disarray.

item.title: Singapore’s Circulate Capital Raises $220 Million for Asia Fund
item.description: Singapore-based investor Circulate Capital, which invests in and finances firms that recycle plastics and create eco-friendly packaging, has raised $220 million for a second Asia fund despite a global downturn in demand for environmental, social and governance funds.

item.title: MSCI Names Greece a Developed Market for First Time Since 2013
item.description: Greece’s stock market was upgraded to developed-market status by MSCI Inc., marking a milestone in the country’s recovery from its decade-long debt crisis.

item.title: Why China’s OpenClaw Craze Is a Global AI Experiment
item.description: On today's Big Take Asia podcast, we look at the OpenClaw frenzy in China: How the powerful AI agent is being embraced by a broad swath of users and what could go wrong

item.title: Carson Block Lays Out Bearish Credit ETF Bets Amid AI Fears
item.description: Famed short seller Carson Block is placing bearish bets on some of the biggest corporate credit ETFs as a way to hedge the risk of economic malaise caused by AI.

item.title: Wells Fargo Unleashes Pent-Up Power in Crucial Repo Market
item.description: Freed from US punishment, the bank has become a stabilizing force in this linchpin of the world financial system

item.title: KPMG Faces Allegations of Blown Audit in Private Credit Collapse
item.description: Canada’s top securities regulator alleged that KPMG LLP, the auditor for four funds managed by collapsed private lender Bridging Finance Inc., failed to properly value the loans held within the funds, harming investors.

item.title: Ark ETFs to Add OpenAI Stake as Retail Investors Chase Tech Boom
item.description: OpenAI is set to gain exposure across some of Cathie Wood’s Ark Investment Management ETFs, the latest test of whether surging demand for pre-IPO stakes can be pushed into vehicles built for daily trading.

item.title: Bond Traders Ditch Inflation Bets as Oil Surge Threatens Growth
item.description: US bond traders are abandoning bets on rising inflation to focus on a potential hit to economic growth from elevated oil prices.

item.title: GFL Environmental Said to Near Buyout of Texas Waste Management Firm
item.description: Waste management company GFL Environmental Inc. is close to a deal to acquire Frontier Waste Solutions, which manages dozens of sites in Texas, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: OpenAI Valued at $852 Billion After Completing $122 Billion Round
item.description: OpenAI has completed a deal to raise $122 billion from investors at an $852 billion valuation, marking the company’s largest funding round to date by far and bolstering its costly push for more chips, data centers and talent.
